result.markdown:
--- title: "Group analysis" subtitle: "Summarize first-level estimates with native group-analysis helpers." jupyter: python3 execute: freeze: auto --- ## Inputs The production seam ends with group-level inference over first-level estimates. This compact example starts from beta estimates and sampling variances directly; flagship workflows pass contrast outputs forward from `fmri_lm()`. Rows are subjects or studies; columns are voxels, parcels, or other features. ::: {#e4fb80c4 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm rng = np.random.default_rng(12) n_subjects = 10 n_features = 6 group = np.repeat([0, 1], n_subjects // 2) X = np.column_stack([np.ones(n_subjects), group]) true_effect = np.array([[0.2] * n_features, [0.4, 0.3, 0.2, 0.0, -0.1, -0.2]]) V = rng.uniform(0.04, 0.12, size=(n_subjects, n_features)) Y = X @ true_effect + rng.normal(scale=np.sqrt(V)) Y.shape, V.shape, X.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` ((10, 6), (10, 6), (10, 2)) ``` ::: ::: ## Fit Meta-Regression `fmri_meta_fit()` estimates one weighted model per feature. It is a matrix-level compatibility helper for the same native group-analysis layer that backs higher-level `group_fit` workflows. Use `"fe"` for a fixed-effect fit and `"pm"` or `"dl"` for random-effects tau-squared estimates. ::: {#2850ff9d .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit = fm.fmri_meta_fit(Y, V, X, method="pm") fit["beta"].shape, fit["se"].shape, fit["tau2"].round(4) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` ((2, 6), (2, 6), array([0.0415, 0.0014, 0. , 0.0182, 0.0043, 0. ])) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#0db49255 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} summary = pd.DataFrame({ "feature": [f"feature_{i + 1}" for i in range(n_features)], "group_beta": fit["beta"][1], "group_se": fit["se"][1], "group_z": fit["z"][1], "p": fit["p"][1], "I2_fe": fit["I2_fe"], }) summary.round(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
feature group_beta group_se group_z p I2_fe
0 feature_1 0.439 0.207 2.119 0.034 0.465
1 feature_2 0.300 0.182 1.652 0.099 0.124
2 feature_3 0.138 0.183 0.751 0.453 0.000
3 feature_4 0.272 0.200 1.358 0.174 0.274
4 feature_5 -0.172 0.171 -1.005 0.315 0.161
5 feature_6 -0.129 0.176 -0.737 0.461 0.000
``` ::: ::: ## Covariance Output When downstream code needs coefficient covariance matrices, use the covariance variant. ::: {#8fc7b6d0 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_cov = fm.fmri_meta_fit_cov(Y, V, X, method="pm") fit_cov["cov_tri"].shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ``` (3, 6) ``` ::: ::: ## Next - [Functional connectivity](functional-connectivity.qmd) - [Benchmark datasets](benchmark-datasets.qmd)
